title: Oppenheimer
logline: The story of J. Robert Oppenheimer's rise as the father of the atomic bomb and his subsequent fall during a security clearance hearing that questions his loyalty, told through two intercut timelines: a color hearing at the Atomic Energy Commission and a black-and-white Senate confirmation hearing for Lewis Strauss.
antagonistOrConflict: Lewis Strauss and the security apparatus that questions Oppenheimer's loyalty; the moral weight of creating the atomic bomb
tone: Cerebral, morally complex, fragmented, relentlessly tense despite bureaucratic settings; oscillates between scientific wonder and existential dread
themes: The moral responsibility of scientific discovery
The cost of ambition and genius
The relationship between science and government
The consequences of creating weapons of mass destruction

actBreaks.description: Oppenheimer delivers his first lecture at Los Alamos, fully assuming his role as scientific director of the Manhattan Project—the mission is now operationally underway.

validation.ruling: Scene #89 (p70, 35.4%) is Oppenheimer's first lecture at Los Alamos where he introduces the scale of destruction they're creating and Serber demonstrates the tiny amounts of fissile material available—this fundamentally shifts the story from recruitment and setup into the operational phase of building the bomb, marking a clear act break into a new phase of higher stakes.

actBreaks.description: The Trinity test detonates successfully, fundamentally transforming Oppenheimer's world and marking the transition from creation to consequence.

validation.ruling: Scene #184 (p131, 66.2%) shows the bomb waking with detonators humming on the tower—this is the final moment before detonation and marks the irreversible culmination of the Manhattan Project, functioning as the act break between the building/testing phase and the aftermath/consequences phase of the story.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This is a triptych structure built on two interwoven timelines that create a prismatic examination of one man's life. The 'Fission' timeline (color, AEC hearing, 1954) and 'Fusion' timeline (black-and-white, Senate hearing for Strauss, 1959) don't just run parallel—they're in active conversation, with each timeline commenting on and recontextualizing the other. The script refuses traditional three-act linearity within either timeline; instead, it uses the collision of past and present to generate dramatic energy. Act One establishes both the hearings and flashes back through Oppenheimer's early life and recruitment. Act Two covers the Los Alamos years and the building of the bomb, while the hearings intensify. Act Three deals with the aftermath of Trinity, the use of the bombs on Japan, and the climactic testimonies that seal both men's fates. The structure itself embodies the film's central theme: how the same events look radically different depending on your perspective and temporal distance.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing is relentless and fragmented, with an average scene length of just over one page creating a staccato rhythm that mirrors Oppenheimer's racing mind. The script accelerates dramatically in the Trinity sequence (scenes 167-199), where 33 scenes cover just 7 pages, creating a rapid-fire montage effect. The hearing scenes provide structural anchors that slow the pace momentarily before launching into another memory. Key inflection points include the Chevalier incident interrogation (scenes 122-139), which uses intercutting between train and office to build paranoia, and the final act's emotional devastation as both timelines converge toward their respective verdicts.

incitingIncident.description: Groves meets Oppenheimer and explains he's been assigned to the Manhattan Engineer District; Oppenheimer impresses him by knowing about uranium procurement and processing, and Groves invites him to see a potential site.

validation.ruling: Scene #49 is a romantic encounter that doesn't shatter Oppenheimer's status quo or establish the main conflict. Scene #62 (p49, 24.7%) is where Groves arrives at Berkeley and recruits Oppenheimer to lead the atomic bomb project, forcing him into irreversible action and establishing the central story goal—this is the true inciting incident that launches the protagonist into the main narrative.

midpoint.description: At a debauched Christmas party, Oppenheimer corners Ruth about Tolman's trip while Kitty in a Santa hat serves spiked eggnog and pretends not to watch Oppenheimer, and he deduces from Ruth's glass that Bohr has arrived.
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. FULLER LODGE, LOS ALAMOS -- LATER

validation.ruling: Scene #143 is a Christmas party that lacks the transformative weight of a midpoint. Scene #145 (p101, 51%) is where Bohr reveals that Heisenberg and the Germans took a wrong turn with heavy water, causing Oppenheimer and the team to realize they are ahead in the race—this is the key revelation and point of no return that shifts the mission from reactive fear to proactive completion of the bomb.

climax.description: Bainbridge peers at the X-unit while Frank and Oppenheimer watch the tower, the electronic counter starts at forty-five seconds, Oppenheimer says these things are hard on your heart, and four red lights flicker on.
climax.sceneHeading: INT. SOUTH OBSERVATION POST, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS

validation.ruling: Scene #260 is Kitty's testimony, which is part of the hearing storyline but not the ultimate confrontation. Scene #183 (p131, 66%) is the final countdown to the Trinity test detonation where Oppenheimer watches the tower as the electronic counter starts at forty-five seconds—this is the peak dramatic tension and the moment where Oppenheimer's work culminates in the ultimate test that will determine success or failure of the entire Manhattan Project.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE OFFICE -- DAY (BLACK-AND-WHITE SEQUENCE)
scenes.summary: Lewis Strauss receives coffee from a Senate aide and discusses Oppenheimer's security hearing, which took place four years ago, expressing concern about whether the committee will ask about it.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, SENATE BUILDING -- MOMENTS LATER (B&W)
scenes.summary: A Senate aide leads Strauss down a corridor, reassuring him that his confirmation hearing is a formality since President Eisenhower has nominated him for the cabinet.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer sits in a small shabby room with three board members and prosecutor Roger Robb, explaining that he went to Cambridge to study under Patrick Blackett because Berkeley's physics department didn't yet exist.

scenes.heading: INT. LABORATORY, CAMBRIDGE -- DAY
scenes.summary: Young Oppenheimer struggles with laboratory equipment and drops a beaker while Patrick Blackett watches; Blackett takes a bite of an apple and tells Oppenheimer to start again, then they rush off to hear Niels Bohr lecture.

scenes.heading: EXT. QUADRANGLE, CAMBRIDGE -- EVENING
scenes.summary: Young Oppenheimer hurries alone across the Cambridge quadrangle.

scenes.heading: INT. LECTURE HALL, CAMBRIDGE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Young Oppenheimer sneaks into the back of an auditorium where Niels Bohr lectures about quantum physics as a new way to understand reality, and Oppenheimer raises his hand to ask a question.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE COMMITTEE HEARING ROOM -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss testifies before the Senate committee that he met Oppenheimer in 1947 when Strauss was an AEC commissioner and board member at Princeton's Institute for Advanced Study, describing Oppenheimer as world-renowned after the war.

scenes.heading: EXT. INSTITUTE FOR ADVANCED STUDY, PRINCETON -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss greets Oppenheimer outside the Institute for Advanced Study, correcting the pronunciation of his name, and Oppenheimer jokes about both their Jewish names before Strauss welcomes him.

scenes.heading: INT. INSTITUTE FOR ADVANCED STUDY, PRINCETON -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Strauss leads Oppenheimer through the Institute, mentioning he admires Oppenheimer's work; when Strauss says he's self-made, Oppenheimer dryly responds that his father was one.

scenes.heading: INT. PRESIDENT’S OFFICE, INSTITUTE FOR ADVANCED STUDY --
scenes.summary: Strauss shows Oppenheimer the president's office and they spot Einstein by a pond; Strauss asks why Oppenheimer didn't involve Einstein in the Manhattan Project, calling him the greatest scientific mind of their time.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer flips a page and continues reading his statement about struggling to visualize the new world of quantum physics.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOMS AT CAMBRIDGE -- DAY
scenes.summary: Young Oppenheimer lies on the floor staring upward, wiping tears from his eyes as he visualizes quantum phenomena and calms himself with memories of horses and apples, then suddenly sits up in alarm.

scenes.heading: EXT. QUADRANGLE, CAMBRIDGE -- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Young Oppenheimer runs desperately against the crowd across the Cambridge quadrangle.

scenes.heading: INT. LABORATORY, CAMBRIDGE -- DAY
scenes.summary: Young Oppenheimer bursts into the laboratory where Blackett and Niels Bohr stand near a poisoned apple on the workbench; Bohr introduces himself and tells Oppenheimer he asked the only good question at the lecture, and Oppenheimer admits he hadn't liked Bohr's answer the first time he heard it at Harvard.
scenes.turningPointNote: Oppenheimer's first direct encounter with Bohr, who will become a crucial mentor; the poisoned apple incident reveals Oppenheimer's psychological instability and marks a turning point in his development as a physicist.

scenes.heading: EXT. INSTITUTE FOR ADVANCED STUDY, PRINCETON -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss watches Oppenheimer hand Einstein his hat by the pond, then approaches as Einstein passes him with a grim expression without acknowledgment; Oppenheimer tells Strauss there are things in his past Strauss needs to be aware of, and Strauss responds that as AEC Chairman he already knows about them.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE COMMITTEE HEARING ROOM -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Senator McGee asks Strauss if Oppenheimer's past concerns didn't worry him, and Strauss says he was more concerned about what Oppenheimer said to Einstein to sour him, drawing chuckles, then acknowledges they all know what happened later.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: The board members listen as Oppenheimer continues reading his statement about moving to Leiden in Holland after Gottingen.

scenes.heading: INT. LECTURE HALL, LEIDEN -- DAY
scenes.summary: Young Oppenheimer nervously prepares to lecture in a packed hall in Leiden where Isidor Rabi sits down expecting to hear a lecture in English, but Oppenheimer begins speaking in Dutch, confusing Rabi who asks the Dutch student for translation help.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN, LEIDEN TO ZURICH -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Rabi joins Oppenheimer on a train to Zurich, offers him an orange, and learns Oppenheimer taught himself Dutch in one semester; Rabi stops peeling his orange in amazement and tells Oppenheimer he should meet Werner Heisenberg.

scenes.heading: INT. LECTURE HALL, ZURICH -- DAY
scenes.summary: Werner Heisenberg turns from the blackboard in a Zurich lecture hall while Oppenheimer studies his every move and Rabi nudges Oppenheimer approvingly.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME -- LATER
scenes.summary: Rabi introduces Oppenheimer to Heisenberg, who says he liked Oppenheimer's paper on molecules; Oppenheimer says he must return to America, and when Heisenberg questions why since no one there takes quantum mechanics seriously, Oppenheimer responds that's exactly why, revealing he's from New Mexico not Manhattan.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer smiles to himself and tells Robb he never met Einstein in person but their paths crossed; when Robb asks if he met any Russians during his time in Europe, Oppenheimer says none spring to mind, then continues reading about accepting appointments at Caltech and Berkeley.

scenes.heading: EXT. BERKELEY -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer walks across the Berkeley campus toward the physics department.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, BERKELEY -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer struggles to unlock a door in the corridor at Berkeley, then opens it.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM, BERKELEY -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer enters a dusty storage space filled with scattered tables, chairs, and a piano.

scenes.heading: EXT. CORRIDOR, BERKELEY -- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer steps out of the classroom and looks at the door next to it.

scenes.heading: INT. RAD LAB, BERKELEY -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer enters the Rad Lab where Ernest Lawrence and students including Alvarez work on a machine to accelerate electrons; Oppenheimer says he wanted to be close to the experimentalists, and Lawrence invites him to a faculty mixer where Oppenheimer might meet his future wife.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM, BERKELEY -- LATER
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer stands expectantly in his classroom as student Lomanitz arrives; Oppenheimer rapid-fires questions about quantum mechanics, explaining that light is paradoxically both particle and wave, and as he teaches, five more students enter and listen intently while he marks papers.

scenes.heading: EXT. BERKELEY -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer strides energetically across Berkeley campus with a group of students following him, discussing Einstein's rejection of the Copenhagen interpretation and Bohr's response.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM, BERKELEY -- DAY
scenes.summary: Lawrence enters Oppenheimer's classroom and frowns at a rally notice for Loyalist Spain on the board; Oppenheimer defends bringing politics into the classroom by comparing it to revolutions in physics and art, but Lawrence warns him to keep it out of the lab and declines an invitation to a discussion group.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE COMMITTEE HEARING ROOM -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Senator Bartlett asks Strauss why the FBI started a file on Oppenheimer before the war, and Strauss says his assumption is it was connected to Oppenheimer's left-wing political activities and how they came to the FBI's attention.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOUSE PARTY, SHASTA ROAD, BERKELEY -- NIGHT (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer arrives at a house party on Shasta Road and spots two men checking license plates; his brother Frank and Frank's date Jackie grab him as he gets out of his car.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, HOUSE PARTY, SHASTA ROAD, BERKELEY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: At the crowded house party, Mary Washburn leads Oppenheimer to meet Haakon Chevalier; Oppenheimer introduces Frank and Jackie, and when Chevalier mentions hearing Oppenheimer is a party member, Oppenheimer denies it while Frank says not yet, then Oppenheimer spots Jean Tatlock across the room and is immediately transfixed.
scenes.turningPointNote: Oppenheimer's first encounter with Jean Tatlock, who will become a significant romantic relationship and later a source of security concerns; also establishes his proximity to Communist Party members without joining himself.

scenes.heading: INT. BEDROOM -- LATER
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer and Tatlock have sex; afterward she climbs off and studies his bookshelves, surprised to find Sanskrit texts; they discuss his background in analysis stemming from trouble at Cambridge when he tried to poison his tutor, and Tatlock jokes he just needed to get laid, then reveals she's a Communist and asks if that's a problem.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW MEXICO -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer leads Lawrence and Frank on horseback across the vast New Mexico landscape up a mountain trail.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAMPSITE -- EVENING
scenes.summary: Thunder rolls as Lawrence climbs off his horse and they set up camp in the wind; Oppenheimer predicts the storm will break before dawn as the air cools overnight.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. TENT -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Frank announces he's getting married to Jackie while they huddle in the buffeting tent; Oppenheimer's sardonic reaction leads to a confrontation where Frank accuses him of disapproving because Jackie's a waitress and because they joined the Communist Party, which Frank sees as Oppenheimer's territory; Oppenheimer says he hasn't joined and doesn't think Jackie should have convinced Frank to either.

scenes.heading: EXT. PERRO CALIENTE -- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer joins Lawrence outside the tent where Lawrence stares at the stars; Oppenheimer describes black holes as gravity swallowing light like a hole in space, then admits Frank has a shitty brother, and Lawrence comments that New Mexico is special, prompting Oppenheimer to say he thought as a kid he could combine physics and New Mexico.

scenes.heading: EXT. STREET, BERKELEY -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer walks with Tatlock on a Berkeley street and tries to take her hand but she folds her arms; a young man bursts from a barber shop with a newspaper, and Oppenheimer leaves Tatlock to chase after Alvarez.

scenes.heading: EXT. BERKELEY CAMPUS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Alvarez sprints across Berkeley campus with a newspaper in hand while Oppenheimer follows.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM, BERKELEY -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer bursts into the classroom where Alvarez breathlessly tells Lawrence that Hahn and Strassman in Germany have split the uranium nucleus through nuclear fission; Oppenheimer reads the paper, declares it impossible, and goes to the blackboard to prove it can't be done while students gather.
scenes.turningPointNote: The discovery of nuclear fission marks the scientific breakthrough that will lead directly to the atomic bomb and fundamentally change Oppenheimer's life and the world; this is the inciting incident for the Manhattan Project.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM, BERKELEY -- LATER
scenes.summary: Lawrence enters the classroom and Oppenheimer points at the board showing his proof that fission can't be done, but Lawrence tells him Alvarez did it next door.

scenes.heading: INT. RAD LAB, BERKELEY -- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer peers at Alvarez's oscilloscope confirming fission, then realizes extra neutrons could split other uranium atoms in a chain reaction; Lawrence confirms they're thinking the same thing, and when Alvarez asks what, Oppenheimer tells him they're all thinking about a bomb.

scenes.heading: EXT. TATLOCK HOUSE, BERKELEY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tatlock grabs flowers from Oppenheimer's hands and dumps them in the trash, telling him no more flowers; when he says he doesn't understand what she wants, she says she doesn't want anything from him, but when he says he'll always answer her calls, she tells him fine but no more flowers and slams the door, leaving him standing outside.

scenes.heading: INT. MEETING HALL, BERKELEY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Chevalier and Barbara lead Oppenheimer into a crowded union hall where Eltenton, a British man who works at Shell, greets them and asks Oppenheimer to speak about organized labor on campuses; Oppenheimer spots his student Lomanitz in the crowd.

scenes.heading: INT. RAD LAB, BERKELEY -- DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM, BERKELEY -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. HOUSE PARTY, TOLMAN HOUSE, PASADENA -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: EXT. PERRO CALIENTE -- DAY
scenes.summary: (no summary)

scenes.heading: INT. TATLOCK’S BEDROOM -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer tells Tatlock he's getting married; she's been crying and tosses aside the flowers he brought, acknowledging it as a door closing though he insists it isn't.

scenes.heading: INT. RAD LAB, BERKELEY -- DAY
scenes.summary: Lawrence confronts Oppenheimer about unionizing the Rad Lab, angrily clearing students out and accusing Oppenheimer of involving them with Communists.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer testifies that he was informed his left-wing associations would not bar him from the atomic programme.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE COMMITTEE HEARING ROOM -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss tells the Senate committee he cannot answer for a security clearance granted before he met Oppenheimer, explaining he asked Oppenheimer to run the Institute because he was the most respected scientific voice.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE OFFICE -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss tells his aide he didn't bring charges against Oppenheimer—a former Congressional staffer named Borden did.

scenes.heading: INT. CONGRESSIONAL HEARING ROOM -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer testifies before Congress with Strauss watching, dismissing a congressman's concern about isotopes by comparing them to shovels and beer bottles.

scenes.heading: INT. OPPENHEIMER HOUSE, BERKELEY -- NIGHT (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer comes home to find Kitty drinking in the dark while their baby cries; she pulls at his clothes as he asks if she needs to go to the baby, and she pushes him away.

scenes.heading: EXT./INT. CHEVALIER HOUSE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer brings the crying infant Peter to the Chevaliers' door and Barbara takes the baby.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, CHEVALIER HOUSE -- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer asks Chevalier to take Peter for an extended period; Chevalier agrees, saying there's a price to be paid for seeing beyond the world we live in.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW MEXICO -- EVENING
scenes.summary: Kitty tells Oppenheimer the world is pivoting and this is his moment—he should lead the atomic project, not Lawrence or the others.

scenes.heading: INT. PRESIDENT’S DINING HALL, BERKELEY -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer notices Colonel Groves at a crowded lunch and asks Lawrence who he is.

scenes.heading: INT. CLASSROOM, BERKELEY -- AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Groves meets Oppenheimer and explains he's been assigned to the Manhattan Engineer District; Oppenheimer impresses him by knowing about uranium procurement and processing, and Groves invites him to see a potential site.
scenes.turningPointNote: Groves selects Oppenheimer as his scientific partner for the Manhattan Project, setting the central collaboration in motion.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN, BERKELEY TO WASHINGTON, DC -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer tells Groves they need to let scientists bring families and build a town for security; Groves asks where.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAR, LOS ALAMOS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer shows Groves the Los Alamos site and explains they'll commandeer a boys' school; Groves orders Nichols to build a town fast and tells Oppenheimer they'll go recruit scientists.
scenes.turningPointNote: Los Alamos is chosen as the site for the atomic bomb project, establishing the physical location where the story's central work will unfold.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN, SANTA FE TO BOSTON -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer asks Groves how much he can tell recruits; Groves tells him as much as he likes until he feels Groves's boot on his balls.

scenes.heading: INT. LECTURE HALL, HARVARD -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer tells Bainbridge and Donald they're needed for isotopes and explosives work; when he crosses his legs, Bainbridge realizes they're unleashing the strong force before the Nazis.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, MIT -- DAY
scenes.summary: Condon asks why he'd leave his family for the middle of nowhere; Groves explodes, asking how about because this is the most important thing in history.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE, UNIVERSITY OF MICHIGAN -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer asks Groves to leave so he can speak privately with a concerned scientist who fears failing a security check will hurt his career; Oppenheimer reassures him they need him.

scenes.heading: INT. QUADRANGLE, PRINCETON -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer tells Feynman the Nazis have the greatest atomic theory minds including Bohr, who is under Nazi occupation in Copenhagen.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN, PRINCETON TO SANTA FE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer asks Groves about getting Bohr out of Denmark; Groves says there's no way until Allied boots return to the continent, then refuses to take a plane because America needs them.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOS ALAMOS UNDER CONSTRUCTION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer shows Rabi and Condon the chaotic construction site; Rabi tells him he's spread too thin and can't improvise this in his head.

scenes.heading: INT. CONSTRUCTION CABIN, LOS ALAMOS -- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer proposes four divisions and offers Rabi the Theoretical division; Rabi refuses to come to Los Alamos, saying he doesn't wish the culmination of his work to be a weapon of mass destruction.

scenes.heading: INT. OPPENHEIMER’S OFFICE, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer puts on his pork pie hat, picks up his pipe, and prepares to leave his office.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOS ALAMOS UNDER CONSTRUCTION -- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer walks the main drag of Los Alamos under construction like a sheriff, nodding at workers—the iconic J. Robert Oppenheimer.

scenes.heading: INT. RAD LAB, BERKELEY -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer watches students in the Rad Lab as workers cover the windows with whitewash; Serber gives him the only key and Teller bursts in wanting to start.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME -- DAY
scenes.summary: Teller interrupts Oppenheimer's summary to share a troubling calculation about chain reactions that causes the assembled scientists to pale.

scenes.heading: EXT. WOODS, PRINCETON, NEW JERSEY -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer finds Einstein walking with Gödel and asks for a private word; Einstein mentions Gödel's paranoia about Nazi poisoning.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKE, INSTITUTE FOR ADVANCED STUDY -- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer shows Einstein Teller's calculation suggesting a chain reaction might not stop and could ignite the atmosphere; Einstein confirms the math is correct and says they'd better be sure.
scenes.turningPointNote: The possibility that the atomic bomb could destroy the entire atmosphere is confirmed by Einstein, raising the stakes to potentially apocalyptic levels.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, BERKELEY -- DAY
scenes.summary: Bethe excitedly tells Oppenheimer that Teller is wrong and gestures for silence as Oppenheimer unlocks the Rad Lab.

scenes.heading: INT. RAD LAB, BERKELEY -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bethe shows Oppenheimer calculations proving the chances of uncontrolled nuclear reaction are near zero; Oppenheimer asks him to run more calculations, and Bethe says they won't know for sure until they detonate one.
scenes.turningPointNote: The atmospheric ignition threat is reduced to 'near zero,' allowing the project to proceed, though absolute certainty remains impossible until detonation.

scenes.heading: INT. OPPENHEIMER HOUSE, BERKELEY -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The Chevaliers arrive at Oppenheimer's house; Kitty jokes about adoption while Oppenheimer tells Chevalier they wanted to see him before leaving for parts unknown.

scenes.heading: INT. KITCHEN, OPPENHEIMER HOUSE, BERKELEY -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Chevalier tells Oppenheimer that Eltenton mentioned scientists could pass information to the Russians through unofficial channels; Oppenheimer gravely responds that would be treason, and Chevalier agrees he just thought Oppenheimer should know.
scenes.turningPointNote: Oppenheimer is approached with an offer to pass secrets to the Soviets—a moment that will later become central to his security hearing.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer testifies that nothing in his friendship with Chevalier would have led him to believe Chevalier was seeking information, and admits he should have reported the incident immediately.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE COMMITTEE HEARING ROOM -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Senator McGee announces a scientist will testify about Strauss; Strauss objects, asking why scientists' opinions matter for Commerce Secretary, and the Chairman says they seek a wide range of opinion for a Cabinet post.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE OFFICE -- MOMENTS LATER (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss's counsel tells him not to act like a defense attorney or the committee will act like prosecutors; Strauss remembers Einstein ignoring him and says scientists resent anyone who questions their judgment, especially non-scientists.

scenes.heading: INT./EXT. CAR DRIVING THROUGH LOS ALAMOS -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer drives Kitty and Peter through Los Alamos as Kitty stares at the basic wooden structures.

scenes.heading: INT. OPPENHEIMER HOUSE, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Kitty discovers their Los Alamos house has no kitchen; Oppenheimer nervously promises they'll fix it.

scenes.heading: EXT. T-SECTION, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Bethe comments on the barbed wire and guns as Oppenheimer walks him through security; Oppenheimer says they're at war.

scenes.heading: INT. LECTURE HALL, T-SECTION, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer presents the Halifax explosion as the biggest man-made explosion in history and proposes calculating how much more destructive a nuclear reaction would be, introducing the term 'kilotons.'

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE COMMITTEE HEARING ROOM -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Senator Bartlett asks Strauss about his disagreement with Oppenheimer over the hydrogen bomb; as Strauss remembers, a siren sounds.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK STREET -- NIGHT (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss's car barrels through New York behind a police escort.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL, NEW YORK -- NIGHT (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss and his assistant rush down a hotel corridor to a door.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL CONFERENCE ROOM -- NIGHT (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss enters a hotel conference room where AEC members are gathered; Bush tells him a B-29 picked up radiation over the north Pacific, and Oppenheimer confirms the Soviets have detonated an atomic bomb.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Soviet Union successfully tests an atomic bomb, ending American nuclear monopoly and triggering the arms race that will define the Cold War.

scenes.heading: EXT. T-SECTION, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Groves returns to Los Alamos and meets the British contingent including Klaus Fuchs, who tells Oppenheimer he's been British since Hitler told him he wasn't German.

scenes.heading: INT. LECTURE HALL, T-SECTION, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Serber explains the 'shooting' method while Tolman proposes implosion; Neddermeyer asks to investigate implosion and Oppenheimer agrees to talk to Ordinance.

scenes.heading: EXT. "MAIN STREET", LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer shows Groves the growing town and mentions he thought of a way to reduce support staff.

scenes.heading: INT. OUTER OFFICE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Groves notices a young woman working at the desk in Oppenheimer's outer office.

scenes.heading: INT. OPPENHEIMER’S OFFICE, LOS ALAMOS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer explains he's offered jobs to all the wives as admin, librarians, and computation staff; Groves objects to open cross-divisional discussions, but Oppenheimer and Condon argue compartmentalization will slow them down.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL CONFERENCE ROOM -- NIGHT (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss confronts Oppenheimer about the open discussions violating compartmentalization; Oppenheimer says they were racing the Nazis, Strauss says now they're racing the Soviets, and Oppenheimer replies only if we start it; Strauss holds up filter papers and asks what's next, and Bush nominates Oppenheimer to chair the committee.
scenes.turningPointNote: Oppenheimer is appointed to chair the committee that will advise on the hydrogen bomb, setting up his eventual opposition to the Super and conflict with Strauss.

scenes.heading: EXT. FOOTBALL STADIUM, CHICAGO -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Wilkins leads Oppenheimer and Condon across a football field, explaining they put the reactor under the stadium since the field's not in use anymore.

scenes.heading: INT. ATOMIC PILE, UNDERNEATH THE STADIUM, CHICAGO -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer visits Fermi and Szilard at the Chicago atomic pile, where Szilard criticizes Los Alamos and Oppenheimer stops a scientist from taking notes about their conversation.

scenes.heading: EXT. T-SECTION, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Hornig, a young chemist, arrives at Los Alamos and Oppenheimer assigns her to the plutonium team after she reveals personnel asked if she could type.

scenes.heading: INT. CYCLOTRON BUILDING, LOS ALAMOS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Groves confronts Oppenheimer and Condon about their Chicago visit, enforcing compartmentalization protocol, and Condon quits the project in anger over the restrictions.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Groves testifies in civilian clothes that he was aware of suspicions about Oppenheimer's liberal background but would be amazed if he committed a disloyal act.

scenes.heading: EXT. T-SECTION, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer greets Lawrence and Lomanitz at Los Alamos and appoints Lomanitz as liaison between Berkeley and the project.

scenes.heading: INT. LECTURE HALL, T-SECTION, LOS ALAMOS -- LATER
scenes.summary: Lawrence defies Groves' compartmentalization orders during a lecture, announcing he will share information he was told not to, and the room applauds Oppenheimer's marble demonstration.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL CONFERENCE ROOM -- NIGHT (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss and Nichols question Oppenheimer about Communist involvement at Los Alamos, and Oppenheimer denies knowingly employing Communists while defending his brother's presence.

scenes.heading: INT. OPPENHEIMER’S OFFICE, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer receives a distressed phone call from Lomanitz and tells him to calm down.

scenes.heading: INT. COLONEL NICHOLS’ OFFICE -- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer confronts Nichols about Lomanitz being drafted, and Nichols reveals it was deliberate due to Lomanitz's union organizing and Communist infiltration concerns at Berkeley.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer testifies that his awareness of security dangers would be greater today than during the war years.

scenes.heading: EXT. HOTEL, SAN FRANCISCO -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer arrives at a San Francisco hotel by taxi, unaware he is being followed and observed by surveillance agents who note the time.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL LOBBY, SAN FRANCISCO -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer moves through the hotel lobby toward the elevators while being watched by a surveillance passenger.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, HOTEL, SAN FRANCISCO -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer removes flowers from his bag and knocks on room 805, where Jean Tatlock opens the door.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION - DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer testifies that he doesn't talk about the secrets he carries regardless of who he associates with.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 805, HOTEL, SAN FRANCISCO -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Tatlock grabs the flowers from Oppenheimer and dumps them in the wastebasket as he enters her hotel room.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Robb references Oppenheimer's statement that he 'had' to visit Jean Tatlock in 1943.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 805, HOTEL, SAN FRANCISCO -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer and Tatlock sit naked across from each other as she confronts him about leaving without a word and he refuses to tell her where he went because she's a Communist.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer testifies that Tatlock was in psychiatric treatment and extremely unhappy, still in love with him, while Kitty watches him describe the encounter in which he appears naked and Tatlock straddles him.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 805, HOTEL, SAN FRANCISCO -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Tatlock confronts Oppenheimer about his power to drop in and out of her life, and he tells her he cannot come again despite her asking what if she needs him.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer testifies he never saw Tatlock again and experiences a flashback vision of a bathtub, pill bottle, and submerged head before jamming his eyes closed.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME -- DAY
scenes.summary: Kitty tells Oppenheimer through clenched teeth that he has said his testimony to history, drops her bag spilling a flask, and Garrison notices it as she quickly gathers her things.

scenes.heading: EXT. BERKELEY CAMPUS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer walks across Berkeley campus and enters an administrative building.

scenes.heading: INT. LT. JOHNSON’S OFFICE -- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer visits Lt. Johnson to ask whether he should talk to Lomanitz and gives Johnson a heads up that Eltenton might merit watching regarding the union concerns.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN, SANTA FE TO CHICAGO -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer tells Groves on a train that he had to meet with someone and there was another man present this time.

scenes.heading: INT. LT. JOHNSON’S OFFICE -- MORNING
scenes.summary: Johnson introduces Oppenheimer to a second man in his office.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN, SANTA FE TO CHICAGO -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer tells Groves the second man's name was Pash, and Groves reacts with alarm that Oppenheimer met Colonel Pash.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Colonel Pash testifies about his surveillance memo documenting Oppenheimer's meeting with Communist Jean Tatlock and states Oppenheimer is not to be trusted on security matters.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN, SANTA FE TO CHICAGO -- DAY
scenes.summary: Groves tells Oppenheimer he would never put him in a room with Pash because Pash threatened to kidnap and interrogate Lomanitz in the Russian manner on a boat.

scenes.heading: INT. LT. JOHNSON’S OFFICE -- DAY
scenes.summary: Pash sits down with Oppenheimer and Johnson, displaying disarming friendliness while expressing interest in Oppenheimer's conversation about someone approaching project members.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN, SANTA FE TO CHICAGO -- DAY
scenes.summary: Groves describes how Pash told the FBI he didn't intend to leave anyone alive to prosecute and warns Oppenheimer that Pash is the man he's dancing with.

scenes.heading: INT. LT. JOHNSON’S OFFICE -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer tells Pash that a man attached to the Soviet Consul indicated through intermediaries he could transmit information, and suggests the commander-in-chief might have arguments for informing the Russians as allies.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN, SANTA FE TO CHICAGO -- DAY
scenes.summary: Groves reacts with shock that Oppenheimer said that to Pash, a man who fought Bolsheviks in Russia and has killed Communists with his own hands.

scenes.heading: INT. LT. JOHNSON’S OFFICE -- DAY
scenes.summary: Pash presses Oppenheimer for specifics about Eltenton and the intermediary, but Oppenheimer refuses to name people he considers innocent, saying it would involve faculty not on the project.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN, SANTA FE TO CHICAGO -- DAY
scenes.summary: Groves stares at Oppenheimer in disbelief and Oppenheimer admits he told Pash a cock-and-bull story to give them Eltenton without opening a can of worms.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer testifies that he did not lie to General Groves and told him he had lied to Pash.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME -- DAY
scenes.summary: Groves testifies that Oppenheimer was under the influence of a typical American schoolboy attitude about not telling on a friend but did what was essential by disclosing Eltenton.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME -- DAY
scenes.summary: Pash testifies that his memo states Oppenheimer sought to provide information to burnish his image as loyal and is not to be trusted on security matters.

scenes.heading: INT. LT. JOHNSON’S OFFICE -- DAY
scenes.summary: Pash presses Oppenheimer about whether Eltenton contacted people directly and through whom, while Oppenheimer insists the intermediary is a faculty member not on the project who ought not be involved.

scenes.heading: INT. TRAIN, SANTA FE TO CHICAGO -- DAY
scenes.summary: Groves tells Oppenheimer he's making a mistake that may haunt him and needs to volunteer the name, but Oppenheimer turns to watch the scenery instead.
scenes.turningPointNote: Oppenheimer refuses to volunteer the intermediary's name despite Groves' warning, a decision that will have lasting consequences for his security clearance.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Groves testifies that Oppenheimer did give him the name, but not at that time, confirming it was some months later.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME -- DAY
scenes.summary: Pash testifies that considerable resources were expended trying to find the intermediary's identity and that he was gone by the time Oppenheimer offered the name, having been sent to Europe.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOS ALAMOS -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer and Serber walk through snowy Los Alamos toward Fuller Lodge, discussing that Tolman has been away somewhere Ruth won't reveal.

scenes.heading: INT. FULLER LODGE, LOS ALAMOS -- LATER
scenes.summary: At a debauched Christmas party, Oppenheimer corners Ruth about Tolman's trip while Kitty in a Santa hat serves spiked eggnog and pretends not to watch Oppenheimer, and he deduces from Ruth's glass that Bohr has arrived.
scenes.turningPointNote: Niels Bohr's arrival at Los Alamos brings crucial intelligence about the German atomic program and marks a significant shift in the project's momentum.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME -- LATER
scenes.summary: Bohr tells a story about being smuggled out and asks Oppenheimer if the bomb is big enough to end all war, not just the current one.

scenes.heading: INT. LECTURE HALL, T-SECTION -- LATER
scenes.summary: Bohr describes his chilling meeting with Heisenberg in Copenhagen and reveals the Germans are focused on heavy water as a moderator, leading Oppenheimer and the team to realize the Germans took a wrong turn and they are ahead.
scenes.turningPointNote: The revelation that the German atomic program is behind the American effort fundamentally shifts the strategic and moral calculus of the Manhattan Project.

scenes.heading: EXT. SNOWY WOODS, LOS ALAMOS -- DAWN
scenes.summary: Kitty finds Oppenheimer curled up distraught in the snow at the base of a tree and he tells her about a woman found face-down in a bath after taking pills and leaving an unsigned note.
scenes.turningPointNote: Jean Tatlock's suicide devastates Oppenheimer, representing both personal loss and the consequences of his choices during wartime.

scenes.heading: INT. LECTURE HALL, T-SECTION, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer vacantly presides over a chaotic meeting where the team argues about plutonium safety, implosion device progress, and Teller's refusal to provide calculations, while the goldfish bowl and tumbler overflow with marbles.

scenes.heading: EXT. T-SECTION, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer confronts Teller at the gate and offers him freedom to research fusion and the hydrogen bomb with weekly one-hour meetings, which Teller accepts before demanding the barrier be raised.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Robb accuses Oppenheimer of trying to deny the Super's viability after the war, but Oppenheimer insists he only pointed out technical difficulties and did not try to kill it at the AEC meeting.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL CONFERENCE ROOM -- NIGHT (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: At an AEC meeting, Rabi draws circles around Moscow and St Petersburg to illustrate the H-bomb's genocidal scale while Strauss points to New York as a potential target, and Oppenheimer hears stamping feet growing louder as he experiences a flashback vision.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE COMMITTEE HEARING ROOM -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss testifies that he never used terms like 'sabotage' but acknowledges Borden did, and Senator McGee questions how Borden obtained detailed access to Oppenheimer's file despite no longer being a government employee.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOS ALAMOS -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer rides his horse through rain-soaked Los Alamos and spots a flier announcing a discussion about the Gadget's impact on civilization.

scenes.heading: INT. CYCLOTRON BUILDING, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer enters a meeting where Hornig argues the atomic work is now humanity's greatest threat, but Oppenheimer counters that the bomb can end the war and save American soldiers preparing to invade Japan.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer walks with Groves discussing progress and costs while Groves notes the population boom at Los Alamos, then spots the heavily pregnant Kitty approaching.

scenes.heading: EXT. CANYON, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: An implosion device successfully detonates in the canyon, Kistiakowsky confirms it's the right design, and Oppenheimer negotiates with Groves for a July test date while insisting he needs his brother Frank on the team.
scenes.turningPointNote: Successful implosion test confirms bomb design is viable and sets timeline for Trinity test

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Robb questions Oppenheimer's judgment for bringing his Communist brother Frank onto the secret project, and Oppenheimer defends that Frank could be trusted absolutely.

scenes.heading: EXT. BERM -- DAY
scenes.summary: Behind a berm, the team watches Kistiakowsky and Hornig arm a detonator that triggers a massive explosion destroying the tower, and Groves tells them to figure out a safer distance quickly before leaving for Washington.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL LOBBY, WASHINGTON, DC -- DAY
scenes.summary: Szilard intercepts Oppenheimer in a hotel lobby with a petition against using the bomb on Japan, but Oppenheimer refuses to help, saying they don't get to decide how it's used.

scenes.heading: INT. SECRETARY OF WAR’S OFFICE -- DAY
scenes.summary: In a meeting with Secretary of War Stimson and military officials, casualty estimates are discussed and Oppenheimer describes the atomic bomb as a terrible revelation of divine power that will have profound psychological impact beyond its physical destruction.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE OF STEEL TOWER, TRINITY TEST SITE -- DAY
scenes.summary: Frank shows Groves and Oppenheimer the Trinity test site layout with observation posts at various distances, and they learn the Air Force wants to practice a bomb drop during the test.

scenes.heading: INT. LECTURE HALL, T-SECTION, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer briefs division heads on the test plan and assigns observation positions while the bomb is assembled and hoisted into the tower, and scientists question the safety distances and radiation cloud predictions.

scenes.heading: EXT. OPPENHEIMER HOUSE, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Kitty notices armed guards waiting outside and realizes the test is happening when Oppenheimer emerges with an overnight bag and tells her he'll send the message 'Take in the sheets' if it succeeds.

scenes.heading: EXT. CANYON, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: The final implosion test detonates and Kistiakowsky shows Fuchs the results on tape with a grave expression.

scenes.heading: EXT. STEEL TOWER, TRINITY TEST SITE -- EVENING
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer climbs the tower alone after the last technician descends and stares at the bomb's detonator-studded surface as thunder rumbles and a storm approaches.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP, TRINITY TEST SITE -- EVENING
scenes.summary: A windmill spins furiously at the army tent base camp as weather worsens.

scenes.heading: INT. BASE CAMP, TRINITY TEST SITE -- EVENING
scenes.summary: Fermi takes bets on the bomb's yield while Groves questions the weatherman about delays, and Kistiakowsky bursts in to report the implosion test failed just as Bethe calls with the same news.

scenes.heading: EXT. STEEL TOWER, TRINITY TEST SITE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: The bomb sits alone on the tower as lightning flashes on the horizon and the last trucks drive away into the night.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOUTH OBSERVATION POST, TRINITY TEST SITE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer and Groves watch rain lash the desert while the weatherman reports continuing bad weather, but Oppenheimer insists the storm will break before dawn based on his knowledge of the desert.

scenes.heading: INT. BUNKER, SOUTH OBSERVATION POST -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Alone with Groves in the bunker during the rain, Oppenheimer reveals they had a moment where calculations suggested the chain reaction might never stop and set fire to the atmosphere, which Groves finds deeply unsettling.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRINITY TEST SITE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Searchlights illuminate the steel tower and a line of lights leading to it comes on in the darkness.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOUTH OBSERVATION POST, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Groves and Oppenheimer emerge into gentle rain as the wind falls, Frank reports the arming party is heading back throwing switches, and soldiers prepare cars for emergency evacuation.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOUTH 1500 POST -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kistiakowsky and military personnel including Bainbridge exit a truck and Kistiakowsky throws a switch on the ground.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rabi, Fermi, and Bethe come outside at base camp and a soldier hands them welder's glass.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOUTH OBSERVATION POST, TRINITY TEST SITE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kistiakowsky and Bainbridge arrive at the south observation post and enter the bunker.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTH OBSERVATION POST, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bainbridge unlocks and throws the arming switches, announcing twenty minutes until detonation.

scenes.heading: EXT. TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A signal rocket flares up into the air.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rabi, Fermi, and others at base camp watch the rocket go up and call out twenty minutes.

scenes.heading: EXT. HILLTOP DISTANT OBSERVATION POINT -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Scientists at the distant observation point watch the rocket, dark glasses are distributed, Feynman refuses them and jumps into a truck cab saying the windshield will block UV, while Teller applies sunscreen at night.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTH OBSERVATION POST, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Groves tells Oppenheimer he's heading to base camp, shakes his hand, and tells him to try not to blow up the world.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME -- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer instructs Bainbridge to watch the voltage meter and abort if it dips below one volt, and Bainbridge nervously watches the X-unit like a hawk.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: At base camp the two-minute countdown begins, observers lie on the ground facing away from the site, and are instructed not to turn around until they see light reflected on the hills.

scenes.heading: EXT. DISTANT OBSERVATION POINT -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Feynman tunes the radio to the countdown relay at sixty seconds while Lawrence joins him in the truck and scientists peer through the windshield as Teller adjusts his dark glasses.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTH OBSERVATION POST, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Bainbridge peers at the X-unit while Frank and Oppenheimer watch the tower, the electronic counter starts at forty-five seconds, Oppenheimer says these things are hard on your heart, and four red lights flicker on.

scenes.heading: EXT. STEEL TOWER, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The bomb wakes with detonators on its surface humming.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTH OBSERVATION POST, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The needle on the X-unit shoots to the right, Bainbridge confirms detonators are charged, and Oppenheimer pulls on welder's goggles.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rabi lifts his head to peek with welder's glass over his eyes while Groves shakes hands with Bush as the countdown reaches eighteen seconds.

scenes.heading: EXT. DISTANT OBSERVATION POINT -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Feynman peers through the windshield and Teller studies the horizon as the countdown reaches twelve seconds.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTH OBSERVATION POST, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: A gong sounds at ten seconds, Bainbridge's hand flinches as the voltage needle bobbles then settles, and Kistiakowsky scrambles out of the bunker as the countdown continues.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOUTH OBSERVATION POST, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kistiakowsky climbs onto the embankment with eyes locked on the distant tower's tiny glow as the countdown reaches four seconds.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTH OBSERVATION POST, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer stares ahead as the counter reaches zero, his breath stops in an agonizing instant before silent light fills everything with full bright daylight.
scenes.turningPointNote: The first atomic bomb successfully detonates at Trinity test site

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Rabi sees sudden daylight, turns to the light, and peers through welder's glass at blinding silent white.

scenes.heading: EXT. DISTANT OBSERVATION POINT -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Lawrence steps out of the car as Feynman shuts his eyes against instant daylight and a hushed intake of breath comes from the crowd of distant observers.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTH OBSERVATION POST, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer's tremulous breathing is audible as the blinding light resolves into a fireball climbing into the sky, he yanks off his goggles and thinks 'Now I am become Death, destroyer of worlds' before the shockwave's wind, dust, and deafening thunder roll over him.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOUTH OBSERVATION POST, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kistiakowsky is blown off his feet by the shockwave.

scenes.heading: INT. SOUTH OBSERVATION POST, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer trembles as terrible beauty gives way to fear while watching the glowing cloud climb to full height with hellish scarlet fire and crackling purple energy, and Frank quietly tells him it worked.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: At base camp startled murmurs give way to crazy cheering, clapping, and dancing while Rabi tries to understand what just happened.

scenes.heading: EXT. SAME -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Ecstatic cheering continues at the distant observation point, even Teller smiles, and Feynman pulls out bongos and plays on the truck hood as people dance to his pagan rhythm at dawn.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOUTH OBSERVATION POST, TRINITY TEST SITE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kistiakowsky grabs Oppenheimer in a big hug then holds out his hand for payment on their bet, and Oppenheimer promises he's good for it since his wallet is empty.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASE CAMP, TRINITY TEST SITE -- MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer arrives at base camp walking confidently and the crowd goes wild, Groves orders an aide to get him Potsdam, and Oppenheimer tells Serber to send Kitty the message to bring in the sheets.

scenes.heading: INT. OPPENHEIMER HOUSE, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Kitty receives the cryptic phone message to bring in the sheets while feeding her baby daughter and young son, freezes as she realizes what it means, and smiles with tears forming as she understands the test succeeded.
scenes.turningPointNote: Kitty receives confirmation that the atomic bomb test was successful, marking the completion of the Manhattan Project's primary goal

scenes.heading: EXT. T-SECTION, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer watches crates containing the bomb being hoisted onto army trucks.

scenes.heading: INT. T-SECTION, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer warns an Air Force Officer not to detonate the bomb too high, but the officer dismisses him and the crate is closed.

scenes.heading: EXT. T-SECTION, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Groves tells Oppenheimer that Truman barely briefed Stalin at Potsdam, and refuses to let Oppenheimer come to Washington or promise to keep him informed.

scenes.heading: INT. OPPENHEIMER HOUSE, LOS ALAMOS -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer sits at his kitchen table waiting for a phone call while Kitty reminds him it's only the 5th in America.

scenes.heading: INT. OPPENHEIMER’S OFFICE, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer paces his office trying to reach Groves when Charlotte alerts him that Truman is on the radio.

scenes.heading: INT. FOYER, OPPENHEIMER’S OFFICE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Charlotte patches the radio to the PA system as Truman announces that an American airplane dropped a bomb on Hiroshima sixteen hours ago.
scenes.turningPointNote: The atomic bomb has been used on Hiroshima — the culmination of the Manhattan Project and the beginning of the nuclear age.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE, T-SECTION, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Frank hears Truman's announcement over the PA system describing the bomb's power as equivalent to twenty thousand tons of TNT.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR, T-SECTION, LOS ALAMOS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Frank and other scientists emerge into the corridor hearing soldiers cheering and banging on trash-can lids as Truman describes harnessing the power of the universe.

scenes.heading: INT. OPPENHEIMER’S OFFICE, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer listens to Truman's announcement and receives a congratulatory phone call from Groves, responding that everybody is feeling reasonably good about it.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOS ALAMOS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer walks through Los Alamos in a relieved daze as people honk horns, cheer, and wave at him.

scenes.heading: INT. FULLER LODGE, LOS ALAMOS -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: A restless, excitable crowd packs into Fuller Lodge like a high-school pep rally.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, FULLER LODGE, LOS ALAMOS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kitty adjusts Oppenheimer's tie in the lobby as they hear dozens of feet stamping rhythmically inside.

scenes.heading: INT. FULLER LODGE, LOS ALAMOS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The impatient crowd inside Fuller Lodge stamps their feet in unison.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, FULLER LODGE, LOS ALAMOS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kitty nods at Oppenheimer and he takes a breath before pushing through the door.

scenes.heading: INT. FULLER LODGE, LOS ALAMOS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer walks past stamping feet that grow faster and more oppressive, then raises his hands victoriously as the crowd cheers and he declares the world will remember this day.

scenes.heading: INT. SAME -- LATER
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer drifts through a silent celebration, experiencing disturbing visions including his foot inside a charred corpse and a young woman crying uncontrollably.
scenes.turningPointNote: Oppenheimer's psychological break as he confronts the human cost of Hiroshima — the moment his triumph transforms into horror and guilt.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, FULLER LODGE, LOS ALAMOS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer drifts through the lobby and sees a young man weeping against the wall while a woman tries to console him.

scenes.heading: EXT. FULLER LODGE, LOS ALAMOS -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer exits Fuller Lodge past joyful revelers and encounters a distraught young physicist bent double with vomit around his mouth.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, OVAL OFFICE, THE WHITE HOUSE -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer waits in the Oval Office lobby and sees himself on the cover of Time magazine as an aide compliments the picture before announcing Truman will see him.

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Truman congratulates Oppenheimer for saving American lives, but Oppenheimer expresses concern about an arms race and says he feels he has blood on his hands, prompting Truman to angrily dismiss him and call for a towel.
scenes.turningPointNote: Oppenheimer's relationship with the government shatters as Truman rejects his moral concerns and humiliates him, setting up his eventual persecution.

scenes.heading: INT. LOBBY, OVAL OFFICE, THE WHITE HOUSE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer walks past the Time magazine cover of himself in the lobby as Strauss narrates that Oppenheimer had fully embraced his father of the bomb reputation.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE OFFICE -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss tells the Senate Aide that Oppenheimer used his profile to influence policy while knowing he was vulnerable, as his brother was blacklisted and Lomanitz worked laying railroad track.

scenes.heading: INT. BALLROOM, PLAZA HOTEL -- NIGHT (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: At Strauss's birthday party, Oppenheimer ignores Strauss's request to meet his son and fiancée, raising a glass dismissively and walking away, humiliating Strauss in front of his family.
scenes.turningPointNote: Oppenheimer publicly humiliates Strauss at his birthday party, creating the personal vendetta that will drive Strauss to orchestrate Oppenheimer's downfall.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE OFFICE -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss describes how Oppenheimer was followed everywhere and had his trash picked through, but never stopped speaking his mind, possibly believing fame could protect him.

scenes.heading: INT. CORPORATE AUDITORIUM -- NIGHT (B&W)
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer lectures that America and Russia are like scorpions in a bottle and calls for candor as the only remedy, urging officials to level with the American people about the facts of nuclear weapons.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE OFFICE -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss tells the Senate Aide that Oppenheimer's candor speech was the last straw for his enemies, and explains that Borden dredged up Oppenheimer's past using his FBI file to destroy his security clearance and credibility.

scenes.heading: INT. LECTURE HALL, LOS ALAMOS -- NIGHT (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer watches Serber and Morrison lecture about Hiroshima using slides showing people burned in stripes and the strange effects of the heat flash.

scenes.heading: EXT. T-SECTION, LOS ALAMOS -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Teller tells Oppenheimer about a British physicist calling the bombings the first act of the cold war, and Oppenheimer agrees they bombed an essentially defeated enemy, then Teller urges him to use his influence for the Super.

scenes.heading: EXT. FULLER LODGE, LOS ALAMOS -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer gives his leaving address at Los Alamos warning that if atomic weapons are added to a warring world, people will curse the names of Los Alamos and Hiroshima, receiving thunderous applause.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE OFFICE -- MORNING (B&W)
scenes.summary: The Senate Aide shows Strauss a Time magazine cover featuring Strauss with a caption stating he fought Oppenheimer and the US won, revealing Strauss planted the story with his friend Henry Luce.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Robb questions Oppenheimer about his influence on atomic policy, specifically regarding isotopes, prompting Oppenheimer to recall his congressional testimony.

scenes.heading: INT. CONGRESSIONAL HEARING ROOM -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer testifies to Congress with humor about isotopes being somewhere between a sandwich and electronic components, making the room laugh while Strauss sits enraged.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer tells the board that he was the spokesman but the opinion on isotopes was unanimous among scientists, and Gray adjourns until tomorrow when Rabi will testify.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM, WASHINGTON, DC -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: In a hotel room, Garrison says it will come down to Teller's testimony when Kitty laughs and insists it's Strauss behind everything, but Oppenheimer defends Strauss for bringing him to Princeton.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE OFFICE -- MORNING (B&W)
scenes.summary: The Senate Aide realizes Strauss gave Oppenheimer's FBI file to Borden and set him on Oppenheimer.

scenes.heading: INT. NICHOLS’ OFFICE, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- NIGHT (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss instructs Borden in Nichols's office to use Oppenheimer's entire FBI file to write conclusions that will force a response, planning to offer Oppenheimer a chance to appeal and appoint a rigged board.
scenes.turningPointNote: Strauss orchestrates the trap that will ensnare Oppenheimer — giving Borden the file and designing the rigged hearing process.

scenes.heading: INT. FRONT HALL, STRAUSS RESIDENCE -- NIGHT (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss opens his front door to Oppenheimer as his voiceover instructs to tell Oppenheimer his security clearance is not being renewed.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, STRAUSS HOUSE -- NIGHT (B&W)
scenes.summary: Nichols hands the indictment to Oppenheimer who reads it while Strauss gives him a drink, and Oppenheimer asks to keep the letter but Nichols refuses.

scenes.heading: INT. NICHOLS’ OFFICE, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- NIGHT (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss explains to Nichols that when Oppenheimer appeals, he will appoint a board and prosecutor Roger Robb who will have security clearance to examine Oppenheimer's file while Oppenheimer's lawyers will not.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer sits at the witness table beginning to give a summary of relevant aspects of his life in chronological order.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE OFFICE -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss tells the Senate Aide that amateurs seek the sun and get eaten while power stays in the shadows, and that Teller and Hill's testimony will help his confirmation.

scenes.heading: INT. LIVING ROOM, STRAUSS HOUSE -- LATER (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer looks up from Nichols's letter in shell-shock as Strauss tells him he'll have to send a copy if he appeals.

scenes.heading: EXT. STRAUSS HOUSE -- CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Strauss gently guides the shell-shocked Oppenheimer down the steps, insisting he take Strauss's car and driver and apologizing that it's come to this.

scenes.heading: INT. STRAUSS CAR -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer sits in the back of Strauss's car with shadows flicking across his face, thinking that Nichols wants him to fight to get it all in the record.

scenes.heading: INT. VOLPE’S HOUSE -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer and Kitty meet with Volpe who says Oppenheimer could walk away, but Kitty insists they must fight to save his reputation and their house, and Volpe agrees to call Lloyd Garrison.
scenes.turningPointNote: Oppenheimer decides to fight the charges rather than walk away, committing himself to the rigged hearing that will destroy him.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Robb questions Oppenheimer about the Pash interview using a classified recording that Garrison cannot access, revealing the hearing's fundamental unfairness as Robb has security clearance to the file but Garrison does not.
scenes.turningPointNote: The rigged nature of the hearing is fully exposed as Robb uses classified evidence that Oppenheimer's own lawyers cannot see.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR OUTSIDE ROOM 2022 -- DAY
scenes.summary: During a break, Rabi tells Garrison and Oppenheimer that the prosecution called Teller and asked Lawrence, who wasn't going to help until Strauss told him Oppenheimer and Ruth Tolman were having an affair.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Rabi testifies that he has known Oppenheimer since 1928 and describes him as a man of upstanding character, loyal to the United States and his friends.

scenes.heading: EXT. CORRIDOR OUTSIDE ROOM 2022 -- DAY
scenes.summary: Rabi gives Oppenheimer an orange segment and stares down Lawrence in the corridor, causing Lawrence to turn and leave without testifying.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- LATER
scenes.summary: Robb cross-examines Rabi about whether Oppenheimer opposed the hydrogen bomb program after the Russian A-bomb test, and Rabi explains there were legitimate concerns about resource allocation.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE COMMITTEE HEARING ROOM -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss watches Teller testify in support of him before the Senate committee, then requests a witness list which the Chairman denies.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Robb announces that Lawrence is ill and calls William Borden to testify instead, who confirms he wrote a letter about Oppenheimer to J. Edgar Hoover without consulting the AEC.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE COMMITTEE HEARING ROOM -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Dr. David Hill begins testifying before the Senate committee with what initially sounds like praise for Strauss, causing Strauss to feel satisfied.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Vannevar Bush testifies that Oppenheimer is being pilloried for expressing honest opinions and that the charges are poorly written, while Evans defends his service on the board.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE COMMITTEE HEARING ROOM -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Hill continues his testimony, revealing that Strauss engaged in personal vindictiveness against Oppenheimer over policy disagreements, particularly regarding isotope shipments.
scenes.turningPointNote: Hill's testimony publicly exposes Strauss's vendetta against Oppenheimer, turning the Senate hearing against Strauss

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Groves testifies that he would not clear Oppenheimer under current AEC rules but affirms he never questioned Oppenheimer's basic loyalty to the United States.

scenes.heading: INT. CORRIDOR OUTSIDE ROOM 2022 -- DAY
scenes.summary: Garrison worries about Kitty's arrival while Oppenheimer defends their relationship, then Kitty arrives appearing unsteady but catches Oppenheimer's eye.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Kitty testifies that she left the Communist Party in 1936 and has been strongly against Communism since before meeting Oppenheimer.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE COMMITTEE HEARING ROOM -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Hill testifies that Robb used legal tricks to trap Oppenheimer into erroneous statements and that he would have protested Robb's tactics if he had been on the Gray Board.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Robb questions Kitty about her Communist Party membership card, she initially freezes while fiddling with her purse, then looks up with steel and deflects his questions with controlled defiance.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY
scenes.summary: Teller testifies that he assumes Oppenheimer is loyal but is asked whether he believes Oppenheimer is a security risk.

scenes.heading: INT. DINING ROOM, OLDEN MANOR, PRINCETON -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kitty angrily criticizes the gentlemanly approach of Oppenheimer's defense team and tells Oppenheimer to stop playing the martyr after he shook Teller's hand.

scenes.heading: EXT. OLDEN MANOR, PRINCETON -- NIGHT
scenes.summary: Volpe tells Oppenheimer he can't win the kangaroo court, then Einstein appears and compares Oppenheimer to a man chasing a woman who doesn't love him anymore—the US government.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE OFFICE -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss realizes the Senate hearing has become a trial about his trial of Oppenheimer and that he doesn't need to prove anything because they're just denying, not convicting.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Robb questions Oppenheimer about moral qualms regarding the hydrogen bomb, asking if the work was just an academic excursion, which Oppenheimer denies.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE OFFICE -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss paces and rants that Oppenheimer wanted to own the atomic bomb and be the most important man, claiming Oppenheimer would do it all over and has never regretted Hiroshima.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Robb confronts Oppenheimer about selecting targets for the atomic bomb and knowing it would kill thousands of civilians, while Oppenheimer hears the sound of feet stamping growing louder.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE OFFICE -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss tells the Senate Aide that Oppenheimer wanted glory without responsibility and needed to be a martyr to absolve himself while knowing the country would continue developing weapons.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Robb asks if Oppenheimer would have opposed dropping a thermonuclear weapon on Japan, and Oppenheimer says he believes he would have, while feet stamp ever louder.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE OFFICE -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss furiously paces, claiming Oppenheimer wanted to suffer as part of his plan to wear guilt like a crown, and that Strauss gave him exactly what he wanted.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Robb gets in Oppenheimer's face, incredulous that he built the bomb then argued it shouldn't be used, while Oppenheimer insists he was just doing his job and giving scientific views.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE OFFICE -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss realizes he gave Oppenheimer martyrdom and the ability to be remembered for Trinity instead of Hiroshima, then asks the Senate Aide if he still has enough votes or faces public humiliation.

scenes.heading: INT. ROOM 2022, ATOMIC ENERGY COMMISSION -- DAY (COLOUR)
scenes.summary: Gray delivers the board's verdict that Oppenheimer is a loyal citizen but his security clearance should not be reinstated due to continuing associations and disturbing conduct.
scenes.turningPointNote: The Gray Board delivers its verdict denying Oppenheimer's security clearance despite finding him loyal—the climax of his hearing

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE OFFICE -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: The Senate Aide informs Strauss that there were unexpected holdouts and his Cabinet nomination has been denied.
scenes.turningPointNote: Strauss learns his Senate confirmation has failed—his public humiliation is complete

scenes.heading: EXT. OLDEN MANOR, BACK GATE OVERLOOKING THE INSTITUTE -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer approaches Kitty who has been crying, and she tells him the world won't forgive him even if he let them tar and feather him.

scenes.heading: INT. SENATE OFFICE -- DAY (B&W)
scenes.summary: Strauss blames the Senate Aide for the failed confirmation and claims Oppenheimer turned the scientists against him one by one, starting with Einstein.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKESIDE, INSTITUTE FOR ADVANCED STUDY, PRINCETON -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer approaches Einstein by the lake and returns his hat, Einstein reflects that a past award wasn't for him but for the scientists, and warns that Oppenheimer won't enjoy his own award.

scenes.heading: INT. CABINET ROOM, WHITE HOUSE -- DAY
scenes.summary: Lyndon Johnson places a medal around Oppenheimer's neck at the White House while Kitty beams, Frank embraces him, and Lawrence claps him on the shoulder affectionately.

scenes.heading: EXT. LAKESIDE, INSTITUTE FOR ADVANCED STUDY, PRINCETON -- DAY
scenes.summary: Oppenheimer tells Einstein he believes they did start a chain reaction that destroyed the entire world, Einstein pales and leaves without acknowledging Strauss, while Oppenheimer visualizes expanding nuclear arsenals.
scenes.turningPointNote: Oppenheimer's final realization that Trinity began the chain reaction destroying the world—the film's devastating thematic climax
